description: Hand-curated, source-verified Slicer code examples. Every snippet uses modern, non-deprecated APIs taken from the official Slicer script repository (Docs/developer_guide/script_repository) and verified against the Slicer source. These are injected as high-priority RAG context so the model copies proven patterns instead of inventing APIs or URLs.

examples.heading: Download a Sample Volume by Name and Volume-Render It
examples.description: Download a built-in Slicer sample (e.g. MRHead) using the named SampleData API (NO hardcoded URL) and enable 3D volume rendering
examples.code:
import slicer
import SampleData

# Download a built-in sample volume BY NAME. Never hardcode or guess a download URL.
volumeNode = SampleData.SampleDataLogic().downloadMRHead()
# For other samples use: SampleData.SampleDataLogic().downloadSample("CTChest")

# Enable 3D volume rendering and apply an MR preset.
volRenLogic = slicer.modules.volumerendering.logic()
displayNode = volRenLogic.CreateDefaultVolumeRenderingNodes(volumeNode)
displayNode.SetVisibility(True)
preset = volRenLogic.GetPresetByName("MR-Default")
preset.ApplyToVolumePropertyNode(displayNode.GetVolumePropertyNode())

# Show the result in a 3D view.
slicer.app.layoutManager().setLayout(slicer.vtkMRMLLayoutNode.SlicerLayoutOneUp3DView)
slicer.util.resetThreeDViews()
print("MRHead volume rendering enabled")

examples.heading: Load a Volume from a File
examples.description: Load a volume (NRRD, NIfTI, DICOM series folder, etc.) from disk into the scene
examples.code:
import slicer

# loadVolume returns the loaded vtkMRMLScalarVolumeNode.
volumeNode = slicer.util.loadVolume("/path/to/volume.nrrd")
print("Loaded volume:", volumeNode.GetName())

examples.heading: Access and Modify Volume Voxels as a NumPy Array
examples.description: Get voxels as a NumPy array, modify in place, and notify Slicer so the display refreshes
examples.code:
import slicer
import SampleData

volumeNode = SampleData.SampleDataLogic().downloadMRHead()

# arrayFromVolume returns a NumPy VIEW in KJI order (slice, row, column), not a copy.
voxels = slicer.util.arrayFromVolume(volumeNode)
print("Shape (k, j, i):", voxels.shape, "max:", voxels.max())

# Modify voxels in place, then notify Slicer so the display pipeline updates.
voxels[voxels < 50] = 0
slicer.util.arrayFromVolumeModified(volumeNode)
print("Volume voxels updated")

examples.heading: Threshold Segmentation Workflow
examples.description: Segment a volume using the Threshold effect in Segment Editor
examples.code:
import slicer

volumeNode = slicer.mrmlScene.GetFirstNodeByClass("vtkMRMLScalarVolumeNode")

segmentationNode = slicer.mrmlScene.AddNewNodeByClass("vtkMRMLSegmentationNode")
segmentationNode.CreateDefaultDisplayNodes()
segmentationNode.SetReferenceImageGeometryParameterFromVolumeNode(volumeNode)
segmentID = segmentationNode.GetSegmentation().AddEmptySegment("Segment_1")

segmentEditorWidget = slicer.qMRMLSegmentEditorWidget()
segmentEditorWidget.setMRMLScene(slicer.mrmlScene)
segmentEditorNode = slicer.mrmlScene.AddNewNodeByClass("vtkMRMLSegmentEditorNode")
segmentEditorWidget.setMRMLSegmentEditorNode(segmentEditorNode)
segmentEditorWidget.setSegmentationNode(segmentationNode)
segmentEditorWidget.setSourceVolumeNode(volumeNode)
segmentEditorWidget.setCurrentSegmentID(segmentID)

segmentEditorWidget.setActiveEffectByName("Threshold")
effect = segmentEditorWidget.activeEffect()
effect.setParameter("MinimumThreshold", "30")
effect.setParameter("MaximumThreshold", "255")
effect.self().onApply()
segmentEditorWidget.setActiveEffect(None)
print("Threshold segmentation complete")

examples.heading: Grow from Seeds Segmentation
examples.description: Create a segmentation using the Grow from seeds effect in Segment Editor
examples.code:
import slicer

volumeNode = slicer.mrmlScene.GetFirstNodeByClass("vtkMRMLScalarVolumeNode")

segmentationNode = slicer.mrmlScene.AddNewNodeByClass("vtkMRMLSegmentationNode")
segmentationNode.CreateDefaultDisplayNodes()
segmentationNode.SetReferenceImageGeometryParameterFromVolumeNode(volumeNode)
segmentID = segmentationNode.GetSegmentation().AddEmptySegment("Region")

segmentEditorWidget = slicer.qMRMLSegmentEditorWidget()
segmentEditorWidget.setMRMLScene(slicer.mrmlScene)
segmentEditorNode = slicer.mrmlScene.AddNewNodeByClass("vtkMRMLSegmentEditorNode")
segmentEditorWidget.setMRMLSegmentEditorNode(segmentEditorNode)
segmentEditorWidget.setSegmentationNode(segmentationNode)
segmentEditorWidget.setSourceVolumeNode(volumeNode)
segmentEditorWidget.setCurrentSegmentID(segmentID)

segmentEditorWidget.setActiveEffectByName("Grow from seeds")
effect = segmentEditorWidget.activeEffect()
effect.self().onApply()
print("Grow from seeds segmentation complete")

examples.heading: Paint Segmentation with Segment Editor
examples.description: Create a segmentation and activate the Paint effect in Segment Editor
examples.code:
import slicer

volumeNode = slicer.mrmlScene.GetFirstNodeByClass("vtkMRMLScalarVolumeNode")

segmentationNode = slicer.mrmlScene.AddNewNodeByClass("vtkMRMLSegmentationNode")
segmentationNode.CreateDefaultDisplayNodes()
segmentationNode.SetReferenceImageGeometryParameterFromVolumeNode(volumeNode)
segmentID = segmentationNode.GetSegmentation().AddEmptySegment("Painted_Region")

segmentEditorWidget = slicer.qMRMLSegmentEditorWidget()
segmentEditorWidget.setMRMLScene(slicer.mrmlScene)
segmentEditorNode = slicer.mrmlScene.AddNewNodeByClass("vtkMRMLSegmentEditorNode")
segmentEditorWidget.setMRMLSegmentEditorNode(segmentEditorNode)
segmentEditorWidget.setSegmentationNode(segmentationNode)
segmentEditorWidget.setSourceVolumeNode(volumeNode)
segmentEditorWidget.setCurrentSegmentID(segmentID)

segmentEditorWidget.setActiveEffectByName("Paint")
print("Segment Editor ready for painting")

examples.heading: Export Segmentation to Model Nodes and Save as STL
examples.description: Export each segment to a surface model under a subject-hierarchy folder, then save the first model as an STL file
examples.code:
import slicer
import vtk

segmentationNode = slicer.mrmlScene.GetFirstNodeByClass("vtkMRMLSegmentationNode")

# Export every segment to a surface model node inside a subject-hierarchy folder.
shNode = slicer.mrmlScene.GetSubjectHierarchyNode()
exportFolderItemId = shNode.CreateFolderItem(shNode.GetSceneItemID(), "Segments")
slicer.modules.segmentations.logic().ExportAllSegmentsToModels(segmentationNode, exportFolderItemId)

# Save the first exported model as an STL file.
segmentModels = vtk.vtkCollection()
shNode.GetDataNodesInBranch(exportFolderItemId, segmentModels)
modelNode = segmentModels.GetItemAsObject(0)
slicer.util.saveNode(modelNode, "/tmp/segment.stl")
print("Exported segment to /tmp/segment.stl")

examples.heading: Load and Display a Surface Model
examples.description: Load a surface mesh (PLY, STL, OBJ, VTK) and set its display color and opacity
examples.code:
import slicer

modelNode = slicer.util.loadModel("/path/to/model.ply")
displayNode = modelNode.GetDisplayNode()
displayNode.SetColor(1.0, 1.0, 0.0)  # yellow
displayNode.SetOpacity(0.8)
print("Loaded model:", modelNode.GetName())

examples.heading: Place Fiducial Markups (Control Points)
examples.description: Create a fiducial point list node and add control points in RAS coordinates
examples.code:
import slicer

# Create a fiducial (point list) node and add control points in RAS coordinates.
pointListNode = slicer.mrmlScene.AddNewNodeByClass("vtkMRMLMarkupsFiducialNode", "F")
pointListNode.AddControlPoint([0.0, 0.0, 0.0])
pointListNode.AddControlPoint([25.0, 10.0, -5.0])
print("Placed", pointListNode.GetNumberOfControlPoints(), "control points")

examples.heading: Apply a Linear Transform to a Node
examples.description: Build a linear transform from a 4x4 matrix, apply it to a node, and optionally harden it
examples.code:
import slicer
import vtk

# Build a transform that translates 50 mm along the R (left-right) axis.
transformNode = slicer.mrmlScene.AddNewNodeByClass("vtkMRMLTransformNode")
matrix = vtk.vtkMatrix4x4()
matrix.SetElement(0, 3, 50.0)
transformNode.SetMatrixTransformToParent(matrix)

# Apply it to a volume (or any transformable node).
volumeNode = slicer.mrmlScene.GetFirstNodeByClass("vtkMRMLScalarVolumeNode")
volumeNode.SetAndObserveTransformNodeID(transformNode.GetID())

# Optional: bake the transform into the data permanently.
slicer.modules.transforms.logic().hardenTransform(volumeNode)
print("Transform applied and hardened")

examples.heading: Volume Rendering of an Already-Loaded Volume
examples.description: Turn on 3D volume rendering for a volume already in the scene and apply a CT preset
examples.code:
import slicer

volumeNode = slicer.mrmlScene.GetFirstNodeByClass("vtkMRMLScalarVolumeNode")

volRenLogic = slicer.modules.volumerendering.logic()
displayNode = volRenLogic.CreateDefaultVolumeRenderingNodes(volumeNode)
displayNode.SetVisibility(True)
preset = volRenLogic.GetPresetByName("CT-AAA")
preset.ApplyToVolumePropertyNode(displayNode.GetVolumePropertyNode())

slicer.app.layoutManager().setLayout(slicer.vtkMRMLLayoutNode.SlicerLayoutOneUp3DView)
slicer.util.resetThreeDViews()
print("Volume rendering active")

examples.heading: Set the Layout and Reset the 3D View
examples.description: Switch the application layout and reset the 3D view camera
examples.code:
import slicer

# Layout constants live on slicer.vtkMRMLLayoutNode (e.g. SlicerLayoutFourUpView,
# SlicerLayoutOneUp3DView, SlicerLayoutConventionalView).
slicer.app.layoutManager().setLayout(slicer.vtkMRMLLayoutNode.SlicerLayoutFourUpView)
slicer.util.resetThreeDViews()
print("Layout set to four-up")

examples.heading: Save a Node to a File
examples.description: Save any storable node (volume, model, segmentation, markups) to disk
examples.code:
import slicer

node = slicer.mrmlScene.GetFirstNodeByClass("vtkMRMLScalarVolumeNode")
slicer.util.saveNode(node, "/tmp/output.nrrd")
print("Saved", node.GetName(), "to /tmp/output.nrrd")
